creator: Kari.Hurtta@ozone.FMI.FI
text:
Cyrille Lefevre:
 > 
 > >Submitter-Id:   current-users
 > >Originator:     Cyrille Lefevre
 > >Organization:   ACME
 > >Confidential:   no
 > >Synopsis:       Updated port: mail/elm+ME - 2.4ME+88 (critical)
 > >Severity:       serious
 > >Priority:       high
 > >Category:       ports
 > >Release:        FreeBSD 4.2-STABLE i386
 > >Class:          change-request
 > >Environment: 
 > 
 > FreeBSD gits 4.2-STABLE FreeBSD 4.2-STABLE #2: Wed Dec  6 17:17:38 CET 2000     root@gits:/disk2/4.x-stable/src/sys/compile/CUSTOM  i386
 > 
 > >Description: 
 > 
 > 	last instant problem while I was making this new release port.
 > 
 > 	yesterday, mi@aldan.algebra.com reports to me that the current
 > 	port of elm+ME (2.4.87) is broken as a lambda user. the current
 > 	work around is to chmod 1777 /var/mail. the symptom is :
 > 
 > 	Can't create lock file! Need write permission in "/var/mail/".
 > 
 > 	this was due by enabling d_savegrpmboxid in config.sh through
 > 	pre-configure script.
 > 
 > 	I didn't see that bcoz I'm always connected as a super-user.
 > 
 > 	maybe this problem have to be submited to the
 > 	-security-notification mailing list ?
 > 
 > 	in this new port, the problem is solved by replacing some
 > 	occurence of setgid(2) by setegid(2) in lib/localmbx.c.
 
 Configure asks:
 
 Can your system use setgid() to switch between the real and effective gid values? 
                     ========
 
 If you have answered to 'y' to that question, then apparently that is not true on FreeBSD. 
 In that case that is configuration error, not bug on Elm.
 
 / Kari Hurtta
